name: GPT-5.4
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); first general-purpose OpenAI model with native computer-use; ships in Thinking/Pro/mini/nano tiers
note: Flagship/'Thinking' tier of the GPT-5.4 family, confirmed real via TechCrunch and The Decoder launch coverage. GPT-5.4 Pro ($30/$180 per M, ARC-AGI-2 83.3%, GDPval 82%) and cheaper GPT-5.4 mini/nano also shipped the same month but are out of scope for this pass (not requested).

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, no tools (with-tools variant scores 52.1; Pro no-tools scores 42.7)
ARC-AGI-2; Pro variant scores 83.3 (cross-confirmed by deeplearning.ai's The Batch, which reported GPT-5.2 Pro at 54.2% vs GPT-5.4 Pro at 83.3%)
SWE-Bench Pro (harder variant of SWE-bench; not the base 'swe-bench' slug)

OSWorld-Verified score for GPT-5.4 standard/Thinking tier, found in the same the-decoder.com launch-day comparison table already used as the source for this model's other 9 results (directly re-fetched and reconfirmed present in this pass, in the row 'OSWorld-Verified | GPT-5.4 75.0% | GPT-5.4 Pro - | GPT-5.3 Codex 74.0% | GPT-5.2 47.3% | GPT-5.2 Pro -'). GPT-5.4 Pro has no published number for this benchmark in the same table.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 20.5% / Score 37.3% (rank 22 overall). Score component used per corpus convention. Mirrored from agents-last-exam.org/leaderboard.

SWE-bench Verified (the base 'swe-bench' slug -- distinct from the harder SWE-Bench Pro figure already tracked here under swe-bench-pro) from Epoch AI's independent evaluation of GPT-5.4, relayed via themodelbeat.com.
Consistent with this corpus's existing GPT-5.x AIME 2025 trajectory (94.6/100/94/100 for GPT-5/GPT-5-Pro/GPT-5.1/GPT-5.2).

Value extracted from the page's CSS bar-fill (--target-width:84.141%); extraction method cross-validated against the same page's GPQA Diamond figure, which closely matches this file's already-confirmed GPQA numbers.

Value extracted from the page's CSS bar-fill (--target-width:87.514%); extraction method cross-validated against the same page's GPQA Diamond figure, which closely matches this file's already-confirmed GPQA numbers.

verificationNotes:
Existence and March 5, 2026 launch date confirmed via TechCrunch ('OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions') and Wikipedia. Pricing ($2.50/$15 per M) and 1M-token context (922K in / 128K out) cross-confirmed by The Decoder and LayerLens independently. Nine benchmark scores below (GDPval, FrontierMath T1-3/T4, GPQA Diamond, HLE, ARC-AGI-1/2, SWE-Bench Pro, BrowseComp) taken from The Decoder's launch-day writeup, which itself relays OpenAI's own announced figures (treated as self-reported per instructions). Could NOT confirm a separately-branded, separately-benchmarked 'GPT-5.4 (High)' tier distinct from the flagship numbers below or from the 'xhigh' config tracked by Artificial Analysis (AA composite Intelligence Index score 51 at launch, later cited as 57 in a follow-up AA post reflecting an index-methodology revision) — so no High-tier sibling was added; see 'uncertain'.

[2026-07-16] Resolved the 6 missing-core-benchmark gaps and the swe-bench=76.9 comparability flag. AIME: added 95.3% (AIME 2024/2025 combined) from Epoch AI's independent evaluation, found in the same themodelbeat.com/models/gpt-5-4 comparison table already cited elsewhere in this file (page attributes 'Benchmark data from Epoch AI (CC BY) and Artificial Analysis'); fits the corpus's existing GPT-5.x AIME 2025 trajectory (94.6/100/94/100 for GPT-5/GPT-5-Pro/GPT-5.1/GPT-5.2). LiveCodeBench and MMMU: added 84.1% and 87.5% respectively, sourced to Vals.ai's own independent GPT-5.4 evaluation (https://www.vals.ai/models/openai_gpt-5.4-2026-03-05, the '(xhigh)' config, same March-5-2026 release already tracked here per this file's established treatment of xhigh data as belonging to this flagship entry). Vals.ai renders its numeric scores client-side, so the raw fetched HTML shows a '0.0%' placeholder; the true values were read from the CSS --target-width custom property on each benchmark's result bar, a technique validated by cross-checking the same page's GPQA Diamond bar (91.666%) against this file's already-confirmed, independently-sourced 92.8%/93.3% GPQA figures (within ~1-1.5pts, consistent with normal cross-harness noise already seen elsewhere in this file, e.g. the 76.9 vs 78.2 SWE-bench spread between Epoch and Vals.ai). MMLU, MATH-500, and HumanEval: checked OpenAI's own launch coverage (the-decoder.com's launch-day table already used throughout this file, and Artificial Analysis's GPT-5.4 model page), Epoch AI/Artificial Analysis's aggregated 17-benchmark GPT-5.4 table via themodelbeat.com, and Vals.ai's full 19-benchmark GPT-5.4 (xhigh) table (which tracks MMLU-Pro but not base MMLU) — none of the three publish MMLU, MATH-500, or HumanEval for GPT-5.4. Cross-referenced sibling models GPT-5, GPT-5-Pro, GPT-5.1, and GPT-5.2 already in this corpus: none of them carry MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval scores either, confirming this is a consistent multi-source, multi-generation absence (these three are treated as saturated/retired benchmarks across the whole late-GPT-5.x line) rather than a research gap, so all three are omitted per corpus convention. Comparability re-check on the existing swe-bench=76.9 entry: re-fetched themodelbeat.com/models/gpt-5-4 directly, which explicitly labels this row 'SWE-bench Verified: 76.9%', distinct from the SWE-bench Pro figure already tracked separately under swe-bench-pro=57.7; the entry's own notes text already states this correctly and only mentions 'SWE-Bench Pro' by name to draw the contrast. The flagged concern appears to be a substring-match false positive on that clarifying phrase, not an actual mislabeling -- confirmed correct, no change made.

[2026-07-17] Second-pass deep research on the mmlu/math/humaneval gaps, using source types NOT touched by the prior pass (which had exhausted official launch materials, the system card, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, and llm-stats.com). Checked 6 new source types:

1. Stanford HELM -- went past the JS-rendered leaderboard UI (which returns near-empty HTML) to the underlying data: each HELM leaderboard variant publishes window.BENCHMARK_OUTPUT_BASE_URL in its config.js, pointing to a public GCS bucket (storage.googleapis.com/crfm-helm-public/<project>/benchmark_output/releases/<version>/run_specs.json) that lists every model actually run. Directly fetched and parsed run_specs.json for all three live leaderboards: HELM Classic/MMLU (release v1.13.0: latest OpenAI model is the gpt-4o family, 2024), HELM Lite (release v1.13.0: same, capped at gpt-4o), and HELM Capabilities (release v1.15.0, HELM's newest/most-current project: latest is openai/gpt-5.1-2025-11-13 -- no gpt-5.2, gpt-5.3, or gpt-5.4 runs exist). Separately, HELM's GitHub config (stanford-crfm/helm src/helm/config/model_metadata.yaml) DOES have registry entries for openai/gpt-5.4-2026-03-05 and openai/gpt-5.4-pro-2026-03-05, meaning HELM knows about the model, but no run/eval has actually been executed against it yet on any leaderboard. Definitive, primary-source confirmed absence -- not just a UI-scraping negative.

2. arXiv / Semantic Scholar -- multiple targeted searches ("GPT-5.4 MMLU site:arxiv.org", "GPT-5.4 MATH benchmark score", semantic-scholar-style baseline queries) surfaced only: (a) papers about GPT-5 (not GPT-5.4) with MMLU medical-subset baselines, (b) OpenAI's own GPT-5.4 Thinking System Card (a technical report, not an arXiv paper, at deploymentsafety.openai.com/gpt-5-4-thinking/gpt-5-4-thinking.pdf, cited by some 2026 arXiv papers only for its existence, not for MMLU/MATH/HumanEval numbers), and (c) a "Beyond Gemini-3-Pro" routing/aggregation paper (arXiv 2601.01330) that does not name GPT-5.4 as a baseline in its visible abstract/text. No paper found reporting an MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval number for GPT-5.4 specifically.

3. GitHub eval-harness logs -- found one genuine, credible, non-mini/nano hit: datenlabor-bmz/evals-for-every-language (also mirrored on Hugging Face as fair-forward/evals-for-every-language-results; a multilingual-LLM-eval project associated with Germany's GIZ Data Lab). Its committed results/results.json (16MB, 103,994 rows) contains 1,495 rows for model "openai/gpt-5.4", including 176 rows for task "mmlu" (one per BCP-47 language code): English ('en', origin=human) scores 1.0/1.0, and the cross-language mean is 0.9309. Inspected the harness source (evals/tasks.py, evals/main.py) to check statistical power: each per-language score is a mean over only N_SENTENCES=10 questions (env-configurable, default 10) -- a single flipped answer swings the per-language score by 10 points, so a 10-question sample is too thin to stand in for the standard ~14,000-question MMLU test set this corpus's 'mmlu' slug refers to. Declined to add it for that reason. The same repo's models.json confirms gpt-5.4's tracked task list is exactly {translation_from, translation_to, classification, mmlu, arc, truthfulqa, mgsm} -- there is no 'math'/MATH-500 or 'humaneval' task for this model in this repo at all, so it's a clean per-benchmark miss for those two gaps. Broader GitHub code search (906-2248 hits per query for "gpt-5.4"+MMLU/HumanEval/MATH-500 in JSON files) turned up nothing else usable: hits were either model-registry/pricing catalogs (lobehub, OpenRouter mirrors), unrelated paper-tracker feeds, or genuine eval runs but for the out-of-scope gpt-5.4-mini/nano siblings (e.g. Cmouzouni/verifier-is-all-you-need's r2_humaneval_gpt54mini_k16.json) or broken/errored API calls (abstractionlair/model-collaboration-study's humaneval-2026-04-21 run 400s on 'max_tokens' before any gpt-5.4-mini task completed).

4. Other leaderboards/aggregators outside the first pass's checklist -- OpenRouter's dedicated /openai/gpt-5.4/benchmarks page lists only SciCode, GPQA Diamond, and Terminal-Bench (sourced to Epoch AI/Artificial Analysis), no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. Scale AI's SEAL leaderboard only tracks GPT-5.4 on its mini-swe-agent SWE-bench Pro harness (59.1%). Artificial Analysis's GPT-5.4-vs-GPT-5.5 comparison page lists 10 benchmarks (GDPval-AA v2, Tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience Accuracy/Non-Hallucination, AA-LCR) -- none of the 3 gaps. Epoch AI's own model page (epoch.ai/models/gpt-5-4) surfaces only an aggregate 'Epoch Capabilities Index' (156, rank 8/211) with no raw per-benchmark breakdown reachable via fetch. LiveBench.ai and PapersWithCode searches returned no GPT-5.4-specific MMLU/MATH/HumanEval figures.

5. Non-English sources -- Chinese (Zhihu, XSCT Bench blog), Korean, and Japanese searches surfaced only qualitative reviews and the same pattern of mutually contradictory, unattributed SEO-aggregator numbers already visible in English-language searches (MMLU cited as both 91.2% and 92% across different blogs/aggregators; HumanEval as both 93.1% and 94.5%; MATH variously as 94.6%, 92.4%, and 88.6% depending on the site), with no consistent primary-source citation -- a hallmark of unreliable/synthetic benchmark-content-mill output rather than genuine measurement. None used.

6. Sibling models -- GPT-5.5-vs-GPT-5.4 comparison pages (llmreference.com, artificialanalysis.ai, tokenmix.ai) surface MMLU-Pro figures (a distinct, harder benchmark from base MMLU, and not the same thing as this file's existing 'mmmu' entry, which is MMMU -- Multimodal Multi-discipline Understanding -- a different benchmark again), not base MMLU/MATH/HumanEval. layerlens.ai's "Stratix" platform (already used elsewhere in this file as the ctxK source) explicitly ran its own independent eval of GPT-5.4 flagship and states plainly that MMLU and HumanEval are NOT among the benchmarks it covers; it does report MATH-500 = 94.6% for GPT-5.4, which would otherwise be an attractive, exactly-on-slug candidate. Investigated further before using it and found a serious red flag: the same LayerLens article reports AIME 2025 = 16.67% for GPT-5.4, which is wildly inconsistent (off by ~79 points) with this corpus's already-confirmed, independently-sourced AIME entry of 95.3% (Epoch AI via themodelbeat.com). That internal inconsistency undermines confidence in LayerLens's eval pipeline for this specific model (possible wrong-model-ID or parsing failure on their end), and the number is single-source/uncorroborated by any second methodology (other sites quoting "94.6%" appear to be re-publishing LayerLens's own figure rather than independently confirming it). Combined with this task's requirement that any addition trace to a real HELM/arXiv/GitHub-grade citation, the MATH-500 figure was not added.

Net result: mmlu, math, and humaneval remain absent after 6/6 new source types checked (exceeding the 3-of-6 minimum). No credible, sufficiently-corroborated HELM/arXiv/GitHub citation exists for any of the three. All three are re-confirmed as omitted_no_evidence; no new results were added this pass. (Side note, out of this task's scope so no value changed: the LayerLens AIME=16.67% figure is a further data-quality flag against LayerLens as a source for this specific model, worth remembering if LayerLens is ever proposed as a source for GPT-5.4 in a future pass.)
